core_facts: Flight 19 consisted of five TBM Avenger torpedo bombers
All 14 crew members were lost on December 5, 1945
The flight departed from Fort Lauderdale Naval Air Station
Lt. Charles Taylor led the formation
A Martin Mariner search plane with 13 crew also vanished

key_figures.name: Lt. Charles Taylor
key_figures.significance: Experienced pilot whose compass failure triggered the crisis

key_figures.name: Lt. Robert Cox
key_figures.significance: First to intercept Taylor's distress transmissions

timeline.event: Martin Mariner dispatched and subsequently lost

suppressed_details: Taylor had requested to be excused from the flight that day
The Navy initially blamed Taylor, then changed verdict under pressure from his mother
primary_sources: Fort Lauderdale Naval Air Station flight logs
contradictions: Taylor was considered experienced, yet became disoriented on a familiar route
archival_gems: Taylor's last known words: 'We'll fly west until we hit the beach or run out of gas'
research_gaps: No wreckage ever recovered despite extensive searches
